summary: Russia launched a massive combined missile and drone strike on Kyiv, causing dozens of casualties and extensive infrastructure damage. Ukrainian air defenses shot down the majority of the 690 aerial threats, including ballistic, cruise, and Shahed drones. Ukraine also reported a successful SBU drone attack on a Russian fuel pipeline hub, igniting a large fire.
key_points: Russia deployed 90 missiles and 600 Shahed‑type drones against Ukraine, including Iskander‑M, Kinzhal, Zircon, and cruise missiles; Ukrainian air defenses downed 604 targets (Source: @UkraineNow/81416).
A combined missile and drone barrage hit Kyiv, damaging over 40 sites, killing at least one civilian and injuring 21, with metro stations temporarily closed (Source: @UkraineNow/81409).
SBU drone strike hit a major Russian fuel‑pipeline station supplying Moscow airports, causing an 800 m² fire (Source: @UkraineNow/81429).
US Embassy warned of a possible large‑scale Russian air attack across Ukraine within 24 hours (Source: @UkraineNow/81407).
Multiple reports of Shahed and other UAVs heading toward northern regions, including Chernihiv and Kyiv, prompting alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67199, @kpszsu/62611).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aerial bombs on Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia regions (Source: @kpszsu/62612, @kpszsu/62631).

Ukraine reported at least 83 casualties nationwide from the night attack, including damage to water supply, market, schools, and a launch of the “nutcracker” system at Bila Tserkva (Source: @UkraineNow/81417).
EU foreign ministers to discuss increased pressure on Russia amid accusations of terror attacks on city centers (Source: @UkraineNow/81430).

sections.executive_summary: Russia unleashed a massive missile and drone strike on Kyiv, causing civilian casualties and widespread damage. Ukrainian air defenses intercepted the majority of 690 aerial threats, including ballistic and cruise missiles. Ukraine countered with a successful SBU drone attack on a Russian fuel‑pipeline hub, igniting a large fire.

points: Ukrainian air defense shot down 11 Iskander‑M ballistic missiles, 44 cruise missiles (X‑101/Iskander‑K/Caliber), and 549 Shahed‑type UAVs during the night attack (Source: @UkraineNow/81416).
SBU drone strike on a Russian fuel‑pipeline station feeding Sheremetyevo, Domodedovo and Vnukovo airports caused an 800 m² fire, disrupting diesel supply (Source: @UkraineNow/81429).
Kyiv suffered damage at over 40 locations, including a five‑storey residential building in Shevchenkivskyi district; 1 dead, 21 injured, metro Lukianivska station closed (Source: @UkraineNow/81409).

President Zelensky reported 83 casualties nationwide from the night attack, noting damage to water supply, market, schools, and use of the “nutcracker” system at Bila Tserkva (Source: @UkraineNow/81417).
Ukrainian forces reported guided aerial bomb drops by Russian tactical aviation on Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia regions, indicating continued Russian air support (Source: @kpszsu/62612, @kpszsu/62631).

Russia launched 90 missiles, including one medium‑range ballistic from Kapustin Yar, two Kinzhal hypersonic missiles, three Zircon anti‑ship missiles, 30 Iskander‑M/SC‑400 ballistic missiles, and 54 cruise missiles (X‑101/Iskander‑K/Caliber) (Source: @UkraineNow/81416).
A total of 600 Shahed‑type attack drones (Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas) were deployed across Ukraine, many heading toward northern and central regions (Source: @UkraineNow/81416).
Russian tactical aviation conducted guided aerial bomb strikes on Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia, demonstrating continued use of precision munitions (Source: @kpszsu/62612, @kpszsu/62631).
US Embassy warned of an imminent large‑scale Russian air offensive that could strike any part of Ukraine within 24 hours (Source: @UkraineNow/81407).
Russian forces reportedly prepared to use the “nutcracker” system, a nuclear‑capable delivery platform, against Bila Tserkva (Source: @UkraineNow/81417).
Ukrainian reports of Shahed UAVs over Sumy heading toward Konotop and Izium, indicating pressure on eastern supply lines (Source: @kpszsu/62657).
Russian tactical aviation dropped guided bombs on Donetsk, suggesting intensified targeting of infrastructure in the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/62612).
No specific casualty figures were provided for eastern engagements in the current set of messages.
Multiple alerts of cruise missiles and UAVs over Chernihiv and Kyiv regions, including a cruise missile over Nizhyn heading toward Kyiv (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67199).
Ukrainian radar reported 2+ cruise missiles from Poltava heading to Cherkasy and Zolotonosha, threatening northern approaches (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67204).
UAV groups observed over Kyiv water reservoir moving toward the capital, prompting civil defense alerts (Source: @kpszsu/62638).
UAV groups from Kherson moving toward Mykolaiv reported, indicating continued Russian aerial pressure in the south (Source: @kpszsu/62645).
No major missile strikes reported in the southern front in these messages, but the SBU drone attack on a Russian fuel hub indirectly affects southern logistics.
Ukrainian air defenses intercepted 604 of 690 Russian aerial weapons, including ballistic, cruise, and UAVs, demonstrating high interception rates (Source: @UkraineNow/81416).
Russian missile launch included diverse payloads: Iskander‑M, Kinzhal, Zircon, cruise missiles, and Shahed drones, showing a multi‑layered air offensive (Source: @UkraineNow/81416).
US warning of a possible large‑scale Russian air attack within 24 hours underscores heightened air war risk (Source: @UkraineNow/81407).

key_developments: Russia launched a coordinated strike of 90 missiles—including Iskander‑M, Kinzhal, and Zircon—and 600 Shahed drones, overwhelming Ukrainian defenses (Source: @UkraineNow/81416).
Ukrainian air defenses downed 604 of 690 incoming aerial threats, achieving a high interception rate (Source: @UkraineNow/81416).
A massive combined missile and drone attack hit Kyiv, damaging over 40 sites, killing at least one civilian and injuring 21 (Source: @UkraineNow/81409).
SBU drone operation struck a key Russian fuel‑pipeline hub, igniting an 800 m² fire and disrupting fuel supply to Moscow airports (Source: @UkraineNow/81429).
US Embassy warned of an imminent large‑scale Russian air assault that could target any part of Ukraine within 24 hours (Source: @UkraineNow/81407).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aerial bombs on Donetsk and Zaporizhzhia, indicating continued precision strike capability (Source: @kpszsu/62612, @kpszsu/62631).
Multiple cruise missile and UAV alerts over northern regions, including Chernihiv and Kyiv, suggest ongoing pressure on the northern front (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67199, @kpszsu/62611).
sections.threat_assessment: Russia is escalating its aerial campaign with a diverse mix of ballistic, hypersonic, cruise missiles, and large numbers of Shahed drones, targeting both strategic infrastructure and civilian areas. Ukrainian air defenses are proving effective but face saturation risks, especially with reported threats of additional large‑scale attacks. The SBU's successful drone strike on a Russian fuel hub demonstrates Ukraine's growing capability to disrupt Russian logistics, potentially limiting Moscow's operational endurance.
sections.regional_response: Western partners, including the US and EU, have issued warnings of further Russian air offensives and are discussing increased diplomatic pressure on Moscow. EU foreign ministers plan to convene to enhance sanctions, while the US embassy's alert reflects heightened vigilance. Ukraine's leadership is calling for accountability and international support to deter further escalations.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of missile launch sites such as Kapustin Yar and the movement of Shahed UAVs from Russian-occupied territories remains critical. The pattern of coordinated missile‑drone strikes suggests a Russian intent to overwhelm air defenses and inflict civilian terror. Continued surveillance of Russian fuel logistics and northern missile trajectories will inform defensive postures and potential counter‑strike opportunities.
